questions.title: How should users hand selection to the pushbutton?
questions.context: Existing ExportToPDA / ExportToPDA_Truss pushbuttons in Analytical.panel/col1.stack/ already follow a pattern. pyRevit pushbuttons can read uidoc.Selection at click time (pre-select) or call uidoc.Selection.PickObjects (pick-after).

options.label: Pre-select required
options.description: User must select elements before clicking. If selection empty → TaskDialog 'No elements selected' and exit. Simplest, matches existing PDA pushbutton convention.

options.label: Pick-after only
options.description: Button always opens a PickObjects prompt with category filter. User can't pre-stage. Slower per-use but discoverable.

options.label: Hybrid — pre-select if present, else pick
options.description: If uidoc.Selection contains valid elements use them; otherwise launch PickObjects. Best UX but slightly more code.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: How should bracings be treated relative to beams?
questions.context: OST_StructuralFraming covers both beams and bracings. Revit's StructuralType enum distinguishes Beam / Brace / Girder. Phase 7 success criteria says columns + beams + bracings are all in scope, but doesn't specify whether bracings get any special handling.

options.label: Treat identically — all OST_StructuralFraming members go through same path
options.description: Single conversion path. Revit's analytical model handles release behaviour. Simplest.

options.label: Tag bracings in revit_meta but convert identically
options.description: Same conversion call, but emit StructuralType in any logged metadata so Phase 8 exporter / downstream tools can route bracings to bar-style elements if needed.

options.label: Separate branch — bracings get explicit pin-release at both ends
options.description: On creation, set AnalyticalMember.StartRelease/EndRelease to release moments for braces (axial-only). More opinionated; might fight engineer intent.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: How should the button behave on elements that are already converted?
questions.context: REVIT-CONVERT-03 mandates idempotency via AnalyticalToPhysicalAssociationManager.GetAssociatedElementId(). The question is what the UX feels like when 8 of 10 selected elements are already converted.

options.label: Silent skip + count under 'already-associated' reason
options.description: Most predictable. Engineer sees '2 converted, 8 skipped (8 already-associated)' in the summary.

options.label: Skip + secondary prompt offering regenerate (delete+recreate)
options.description: After running, if any were skipped as already-associated, ask 'Regenerate 8 already-converted members?' Risky — destroys engineer's analytical edits.

options.label: Skip but make 'already-associated' a distinct line in summary, not lumped with errors
options.description: Like (a) but visually separates 'no-op skips' from 'genuine skips' (unsupported category, missing data) in the TaskDialog.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Where does the configurable supported-category list live?
questions.context: REVIT-CONVERT-01 mandates the category filter is a configurable list, NOT hard-coded — so v1.5+ Phase 15 (slabs/foundations) can extend without rewiring. Windows host uses manual-copy deploy (per memory), so changing code requires re-copy.

options.label: Module-level constant at top of script.py (SUPPORTED_CATEGORIES = [...])
options.description: Easiest. v1.5+ adds OST_Floors to the list. Requires code change + Windows re-deploy to alter.

options.label: JSON sibling file (categories.json) loaded at button click
options.description: User-editable on Windows host without git/code-change. More flexible but adds a load+parse step + error handling.

options.label: Module-level dict mapping category → handler function
options.description: Constant + dispatch table in one. v1.5+ adds a key + a handler. Forces handler-per-category architecture early — useful when slabs need different conversion path.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Should v1.3 introduce the per-category-handler pattern, or defer until v1.5+ actually needs it?
questions.context: Q-04(c) bakes in a strategy/dispatch pattern now. v1.3 only handles columns + beams + bracings — all via Document.GenerateMembersFromSelection. The seed for slabs (SEED-001) confirms slabs will need a different conversion path (tributary-area logic).

options.label: Defer — single function for v1.3, refactor in v1.5+
options.description: YAGNI. One conversion function for all v1.3 categories. Refactor when slabs land. CLAUDE.md hard rule favours this (no abstraction beyond what task requires).

options.label: Adopt now — even though v1.3 has one handler, structure code as dispatch from day one
options.description: Avoids retrofitting in v1.5+. ~20 extra lines of code now.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: What transaction structure wraps the batch conversion?
questions.context: Revit requires every doc-modifying operation inside a Transaction. Success criterion says 'a single bad element does not abort the whole batch' (REVIT-CONVERT-04). Three structural choices, all valid in Revit API.

options.label: Single Transaction wrapping the whole loop, try/except per element, continue on error
options.description: Simplest. Failed elements are logged but don't roll back successful ones (Revit allows continued ops within a transaction after a caught exception, generally). Matches existing ExportToPDA pattern if it follows this.

options.label: TransactionGroup wrapping per-element Transactions
options.description: Each element gets its own Transaction, all under one named TransactionGroup. Cleaner undo (one undo step) and per-element rollback on failure. More API ceremony.

options.label: Per-element SubTransaction inside one outer Transaction
options.description: Outer Transaction commits atomically; sub-transactions allow per-element rollback within. Niche.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: How granular should skip-reason taxonomy be in REVIT-CONVERT-04?
questions.context: Success criterion calls out 'already-associated, unsupported category, missing physical-side data' as example reasons. The set could be tighter or broader.

options.label: 3 fixed reasons exactly: already-associated, unsupported-category, missing-physical-data
options.description: Predictable. Anything else collapses into a single 'other-error' bucket with the exception message.

options.label: Open-ended — exception type or message becomes the reason
options.description: Most flexible. Engineer sees actual API errors verbatim. Less polished UX.

options.label: Curated short list + 'other-error' fallback with logged detail
options.description: Defined enum (already-associated, unsupported-category, missing-location, missing-section, generation-failed, other-error) — 'other-error' carries the raw message. Compromise between polish and visibility.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: What format should the post-run diagnostic surface use?
questions.context: Success criterion calls for 'TaskDialog summary {converted: N, skipped: M, total: N+M} with per-skip reasons'. pyRevit also offers an Output Window (output.print_md) with clickable element links — used by other PDA pushbuttons?

options.label: Single TaskDialog with summary text + expandable detail content
options.description: TaskDialog.ExpandedContent fits per-element rows. Native Revit feel. Element IDs are text only (not clickable).

options.label: TaskDialog summary + pyRevit Output Window with clickable element links for skips
options.description: Best UX — engineer can click 'skipped: ID 12345' and Revit highlights it. Two surfaces for one event.

options.label: Output Window only (markdown table)
options.description: Modern pyRevit pattern. Slight UX regression vs. modal TaskDialog (engineer might miss it). Native to pyRevit, no Revit-version coupling.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: On a fully-clean run (all elements converted, zero skipped), what's the UX?
questions.context: Engineer just selected 50 columns and clicked the button. All 50 converted. Should they get a TaskDialog confirming, or silent success?

options.label: Always show TaskDialog with the summary, even on full success
options.description: Confidence-affirming. Engineer sees 'converted: 50, skipped: 0' explicitly.

options.label: Silent on full success, TaskDialog only when there are skips
options.description: Less interruptive. Engineer assumes success unless told otherwise.

options.label: Brief pyRevit toast/balloon on full success, TaskDialog when skips exist
options.description: Best of both — non-modal acknowledgement + modal only when needed.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: How much should we trust Document.GenerateMembersFromSelection to preserve section/material/parameters?
questions.context: REVIT-CONVERT-02 says the API 'preserves Revit's section / material / parameter associations to the physical element'. In practice it's mostly correct but some edge cases (slanted columns, cropped beams) lose data.

options.label: Trust the API — no post-creation verification
options.description: Simplest. Failures surface in Phase 8 export when section data is missing. Faster v1.3 delivery.

options.label: Post-creation read-back: verify each created AnalyticalMember has a non-null section + material; flag mismatches as 'missing-section' skip
options.description: Defensive. Catches edge cases at conversion time, not three phases later. ~10 extra lines per element.

options.label: Read-back + explicit re-set: if section missing, re-apply from physical element via parameter copy
options.description: Belt-and-braces. Risk of fighting Revit's analytical-physical association manager.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Fallback path — when Document.GenerateMembersFromSelection skips an element, do we attempt manual creation via AnalyticalToPhysicalAssociationManager.AddAssociation?
questions.context: REVIT-CONVERT-02 says 'fall back to per-element creation via AnalyticalToPhysicalAssociationManager.AddAssociation for cases the built-in skips'. This is more API surface and more failure modes.

options.label: Yes — implement fallback in v1.3 as spec'd
options.description: Honours requirement literally. Increases coverage at cost of extra code path + UAT cases.

options.label: Defer fallback — log skipped-by-built-in as 'generation-failed', let engineer fix manually
options.description: Pragmatic. Most v1.3 UAT models won't trigger the edge case. Add fallback in v1.4+ if real models need it.

options.label: Yes but as a separate Plan 4 (so it can be deferred without re-scoping)
options.description: Bake into the 3-plan structure as the third plan, so dropping it cleanly truncates scope if time-constrained.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: What's the UAT fixture strategy on the Windows host?
questions.context: Phase 7 needs Windows-host UAT (Revit 2025+). Manual-copy deploy from Mac dev → Windows. v1.2 Phase 5 used a hand-crafted RVT model. SEED for shared lib + UAT pattern exists.

options.label: Hand-crafted single RVT — simple frame: 4 columns + beams + diagonal brace
options.description: Fastest. One model, covers all 3 categories. Engineer-judged pass/fail.

options.label: 3 RVT fixtures — minimal frame, multi-storey, idempotency re-run case
options.description: Hits all success criteria explicitly. More setup.

options.label: 1 RVT fixture + a TestCodes.panel/RegressionTest.pushbutton that runs the convert + asserts counts
options.description: Reproducible. Asserts {converted, skipped, total} programmatically. More code, cleaner regression story.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Phase 8 (Tier 2 export) needs analytical members to read. How tight is Phase 7's exit gate to Phase 8's entry?
questions.context: Roadmap says Phase 8 'Depends on: Phase 7 (analytical members must exist to be exported in UAT)'. Question is whether Phase 7 closes the loop with a Phase 5 round-trip on its own output, or stops at 'analytical members exist'.

options.label: Phase 7 closes when analytical members exist (success criteria 1-3 only)
options.description: Tight scope. Phase 8 picks up immediately. Round-trip validation lives in Phase 8.

options.label: Phase 7 also runs the existing Phase 5/v1.2 frame2d round-trip on its converted output
options.description: Success criterion 4 says exactly this — runs the existing Tier 1 exporter on the converted analytical model. Catches conversion bugs that would otherwise surface in Phase 8.

options.label: Phase 7 closes after (b) AND a fresh Phase 8-style smoke export (no UI exit until both work)
options.description: Strongest gate. Risks Phase 7 sliding into Phase 8 territory.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: How should the 3 plans split?
questions.context: ROADMAP says 3 plans for Phase 7. Sibling-repo work, IronPython 2.7, manual-copy Windows deploy.

options.label: Skeleton → Conversion logic → Diagnostics+UAT
options.description: Plan 1: pushbutton bundle + selection filter + idempotency check. Plan 2: per-element conversion (built-in API + optional fallback) + transaction. Plan 3: TaskDialog/output summary + UAT fixtures + Windows deploy verification.

options.label: Bundle+Selection → Conversion+Idempotency → Diagnostics+UAT
options.description: Plan 1: pushbutton bundle + uidoc.Selection wiring + category filter (no conversion yet). Plan 2: GenerateMembersFromSelection + association-manager idempotency + transaction. Plan 3: TaskDialog summary + per-skip reasons + UAT.

options.label: Conversion-core → Idempotency+Diagnostics → UAT-only
options.description: Plan 1: skeleton + conversion (no idempotency). Plan 2: idempotency + diagnostic UX. Plan 3: pure UAT + Windows deploy verification.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Should shared logic land in a new lib/Snippets/ helper this phase, or stay inline in script.py?
questions.context: Phase 8 plans an Analytical.extension/lib/Snippets/_pda_export_common.py. Phase 7 introduces selection-filter and category-handling code that's likely reusable. lib/Snippets/ already exists with _selection_func.py, _units_conversion.py.

options.label: Inline in ConvertToAnalytical.pushbutton/script.py for v1.3
options.description: YAGNI. Move to Snippets in Phase 8 when actual sharing happens. Avoids premature abstraction (CLAUDE.md hard rule).

options.label: Extract category-filter helper to lib/Snippets/_analytical_categories.py now
options.description: Pre-positions for Phase 8. Risk: Phase 8's needs may diverge.

options.label: Extend existing _selection_func.py with category-aware filter
options.description: Builds on what's there. Tightest cohesion — selection logic in one place.

options.label: Custom
